commandsRun: rm -rf turbo cache && DATABASE_URL=... pnpm test (x2 cold)
implReport.notes: Pure fns: isEmailOpenType, isPurchaseLike, extractAmount (finite, default 0, never NaN), applyEventToFeatures (total+=1; last_event_at MAX; last_email_open_at MAX only on open; per-type counters; monetary += amount purchase-like). buildFeatureUpsert = single CTE: WITH ins AS (INSERT events ON CONFLICT(event_id) DO NOTHING RETURNING) INSERT profile_features SELECT ... AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM ins) ON CONFLICT(profile_id) DO UPDATE mirroring applyEventToFeatures; workspace_id $1; profile_id via (workspace_id,external_id) subquery; open-ts NULL for non-open so GREATEST preserves. planProcessing now [profileUpsert, eventInsert+featureUpsert CTE] so gate can't drift. ProfileFeatures type + OPEN/PURCHASE constants in shared. Idempotency proven: replay leaves aggregates unchanged, one events row. Segment eval (Phase 5) left as extension point. Fixed two cross-file collisions during cold run: added DELETE profile_features to existing cleanups (FK), and re-namespaced event_ids (global PK) so parallel integration files don't collide. turbo cache:false retained.
implReport.testSummary: Cold pnpm test run TWICE (cache cleared): both green, 28/28 turbo tasks, 0 cached. Integration tier ran on real PG. Processor 47 passed + 1 skipped (fifo-localstack e2e). New: features-core 20 unit; features-aggregate 5 (AC1 rollups + replay no-double-count on real PG); features-workspace-scoping 2 (AC2 in-code scoping on BYPASSRLS pool). Phase 2/3 tests still pass.

invariants: Tenant isolation is the top property: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id.
Service-role Lambda code bypasses RLS — it MUST scope by workspace_id in code; RLS is only the backstop for user-context (admin app) reads.
workspace_id is NEVER taken from a client payload — derive it from the API key (ingest) or the authorizer-injected claim (admin API).
Per-profile ordering via MessageGroupId=profile_id; code must be idempotent and order-convergent (progress-before-profile_created still converges).
Idempotency: events.event_id is the dedupe key (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
Segment rule-AST -> SQL is compiled with a field/operator whitelist, parameterized SQL only, and ALWAYS prepends workspace_id = $ws. Never interpolate raw input.
Local-only: verify against LocalStack + Supabase CLI/Testcontainers. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier. Mock SES (never send real mail).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited spec sections require — no extra features, no gold-plating.

criteria: Feature aggregates update correctly and idempotently as events are processed (replaying an event does not double-count).
profile_features rows are always workspace-scoped and never mix workspaces.
scope: processor feature-update step: total_events, last_event_at, last_email_open_at, counters (jsonb rolling aggregates), monetary_total — all workspace-scoped, idempotent under event replay.
phase.title: Profiles + features

implUnits: buildFeatureUpsert: single CTE WITH ins AS (INSERT events ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING) INSERT profile_features SELECT WHERE EXISTS(ins) ON CONFLICT(profile_id) DO UPDATE total+1, last_event_at=GREATEST, last_email_open_at=GREATEST, counters jsonb increment, monetary+EXCLUDED
wire into planProcessing replacing standalone event insert with combined CTE, after profile upsert
plan.notes: Idempotency = option (a) gate feature update on event being NEW in same tx. Since runPlanInWorkspaceTx runs statements separately (no JS RETURNING handoff), gate must live INSIDE feature SQL via single CTE: WITH ins AS (INSERT events ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING event_id) INSERT profile_features ... WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM ins) ON CONFLICT(profile_id) DO UPDATE. Replay -> ins empty -> no-op. Merges event insert + feature upsert into ONE statement, after buildProcessorProfileUpsert (keeps profile row for FKs). Workspace scoping: workspace_id $1 + (workspace_id,external_id) subquery, never client profile_id; holds on BYPASSRLS pool. SQL mirrors applyEventToFeatures: total+=1; last_event_at=GREATEST(existing,occurred_at); last_email_open_at=GREATEST only when open (NULL otherwise so GREATEST preserves); counters jsonb per-type COALESCE+1; monetary+=extractAmount for purchase-like. Reuse adminPool/hasDatabaseUrl + runPlanInWorkspaceTx; unique workspace UUID namespace per file; cleanup also DELETE profile_features. Excludes segment re-eval (Phase 5 extension point).

assertions: applyEventToFeatures(null,progress@T1)->total=1,last_event_at=T1,counters={progress:1}
purchase@T2 amount30->total=2,last_event_at=T2,monetary=30
last_event_at=MAX (older event doesn't regress)
isEmailOpenType open types; last_email_open_at set+MAX on open only
isPurchaseLike+extractAmount; missing amount->0 no NaN
per-type counter increment; unknown type ->1
buildFeatureUpsert text INSERT...ON CONFLICT(profile_id) DO UPDATE gated WHERE EXISTS(event-insert CTE)
binds workspace_id $1, profile_id via (workspace_id,external_id) subquery
feature step after event insert
[progress@T1,email_open@T2,purchase@T3 amt40]->one row total=3,last_event_at=T3,last_email_open_at=T2,counters all 1,monetary=40
REPLAY same event_id->unchanged (total 3,monetary 40,counters.purchase 1)
events has one row for replayed id
older occurred_at doesn't regress last_event_at end-to-end
exactly one profile_features row per profile
two ws same external_id -> two distinct feature rows, no cross-bleed
workspace_id matches processing ws; count WHERE ws=A returns A only
A totals/counters/monetary reflect only A events
runs on admin BYPASSRLS pool proving in-code isolation

instructions: Independently RUN the tests (pnpm test for the phase scope), pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint. Record real pass/fail.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is actually covered by a passing test: ["Feature aggregates update correctly and idempotently as events are processed (replaying an event does not double-count).","profile_features rows are always workspace-scoped and never mix workspaces."].

Tenant-isolation review (highest priority): confirm workspace_id scoping in code on every query, that RLS policies are correct, that service-role paths still scope in code, and that workspace_id is never read from client input. For phase5, verify the SQL compiler whitelists fields/operators, parameterizes, and ALWAYS injects workspace_id.
Scope check: flag any feature implemented beyond the cited spec sections.
If integration tests mock Postgres, that is a FAIL (must use real local Postgres per §16A).
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met:boolean, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity and tenant isolation
prompt.task: Independently verify "Profiles + features" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself; do not trust the self-report.
